events.description: SAVE THE DATE: Sunstock Solar Powered Music Festival and Sustainability Fair is April 19 from 12-7 PM on West Crescent! Sunstock is 100% free for anyone to join! We will have free arts and crafts, local organizations tabling, and 6 bands performing throughout the day. West Crescent is located on Center St across from BAMPFA. We are so excited to see you all there!

events.title: Human Rights and Democracy in Cambodia: Its Past, Present, and Future
events.organizer: Alexander Hamilton Society at Berkeley (Formerly known as Alexander Hamilton Society, The)
events.description: Join us on April 22 from 12:30–2:00 PM for “Human Rights and Democracy in Cambodia: Its Past, Present, and Future.” This event will feature Mu Sochua (via Zoom), a Renew Democracy Initiative Frontline Fellow and longtime Cambodian opposition leader and human rights activist, alongside Soknin Chhoeun, a youth leader and advocate for gender equality, civic engagement, and social justice in Cambodia. The discussion will be moderated by Jessica Olney, Executive Director of IIS. The event will take place in Philosophy Hall, Room 223, and lunch will be provided.

events.title: Spring Trans Healthcare Panel
events.organizer: Campus Dept | UC Berkeley Gender Equity Resource Center
events.location: Gender Equity Resource Center, 202 Chavez Student Center
events.description: A comprehensive Q&A panel with many helpful perspectives and local providers involved in surgical gender affirming procedures, including surgeons, insurance experts, mental health providers, and students. Food Provided! Please RSVP at TSWI RSVP Form (Spring 2026) This event is open to all UC Berkeley community members, regardless of race, color, national origin, ethnicity, age, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, religion, or any other protected class status. UC Berkeley is committed to fostering an inclusive environment that welcomes participants from all backgrounds. If you require an accommodation for effective communication or information about campus mobility access features in order to fully participate in this event, please contact Joanna Villegas at (510) 230-3254 or joannavillegas @‌berkeley.edu with as much advance notice as possible and at least 7-10 days in advance of the event.

events.title: The Sweet Spot: Sex Ed Zines & Peer-Discussion
events.organizer: Campus Dept | UC Berkeley Gender Equity Resource Center
events.location: Gender Equity Resource Center 2nd floor, 202 Student Center, Suite 202
events.description: Let’s talk about what traditional health education often leaves out. The Sweet Spot: Sex ed Zines & Peer Discussion is a drop in, peer-led space centered on gender, queerness, and lived experiences around relationships, intimacy, and identity. Rather than a structured workshop, this program invites participants to engage at their own pace; whether that means exploring zines, joining conversations, or simply being present in the space. This space will also feature zines created by GenEq interns and community zine distributors, alongside accessible wellness and sexual health resources; all shared in a low pressure, affirming environment!
